question: True or false: Every square is a rectangle.
hints: A rectangle needs 4 right angles and opposite sides equal. Does a square have these?
solution: True. A square has 4 right angles and opposite sides are equal, so it is a rectangle.

question: True or false: Every rectangle is a parallelogram.
hints: A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides.
solution: True. A rectangle has 2 pairs of parallel sides, so it is a parallelogram.

question: True or false: Every parallelogram is a rectangle.
hints: Does every parallelogram have 4 right angles?
solution: False. A parallelogram only needs 2 pairs of parallel sides. It does not need right angles. Only special parallelograms (rectangles) have right angles.

question: Is a rhombus a parallelogram?
hints: Does a rhombus have 2 pairs of parallel sides?
solution: Yes. A rhombus has 2 pairs of parallel sides, so it is a parallelogram.

question: Which is more specific: quadrilateral or parallelogram?
hints: Every parallelogram is a quadrilateral, but not every quadrilateral is a parallelogram.
solution: Parallelogram is more specific. All parallelograms are quadrilaterals, but not all quadrilaterals are parallelograms (e.g., trapezoids).

question: Put these in order from most general to most specific: square, quadrilateral, parallelogram, rectangle.
hints: Start with the shape that includes all the others.
solution: Quadrilateral (most general) → parallelogram → rectangle → square (most specific). Each is a special case of the one before it.

question: A shape has 4 sides, 2 pairs of parallel sides, and no right angles. All sides are different lengths. Is it a parallelogram?
hints: A parallelogram requires opposite sides to be equal.
solution: No. A parallelogram must have opposite sides equal and parallel. If all sides are different lengths, opposite sides cannot be equal, so it is not a parallelogram. It would be a general quadrilateral (or possibly a trapezoid if it has at least one pair of parallel sides).

question: Name all the categories a square belongs to from this list: quadrilateral, parallelogram, rectangle, rhombus, trapezoid.
hints: Check each definition against the properties of a square.
solution: A square is a quadrilateral (4 sides), parallelogram (2 pairs of parallel sides), rectangle (4 right angles), and rhombus (4 equal sides). It is not typically classified as a trapezoid in the exclusive definition.

question: True or false: A trapezoid is a parallelogram.
hints: A trapezoid has exactly one pair of parallel sides. A parallelogram has two.
solution: False. A trapezoid has exactly one pair of parallel sides, while a parallelogram requires two pairs.

question: A shape is a rhombus with 4 right angles. What is the most specific name for this shape?
hints: A rhombus with right angles has both 4 equal sides and 4 right angles.
solution: A rhombus with 4 right angles is a square. 'Square' is the most specific name.

question: Give the most specific name: a quadrilateral with 2 pairs of parallel sides, 4 right angles, and sides of 5 cm and 8 cm.
hints: Are all sides equal? If not, it can't be a square.
solution: It is a rectangle. It has right angles and parallel sides, but its sides are not all equal (5 cm and 8 cm), so it is not a square or rhombus.

question: Why is a square always a rhombus, but a rhombus is not always a square?
answer: A square has 4 equal sides (making it a rhombus), but a rhombus does not need right angles (which a square requires)
hints: Compare the requirements: what does a square need that a rhombus doesn't?
solution: A square has 4 equal sides, satisfying the rhombus definition. But a rhombus only requires 4 equal sides — it doesn't need right angles. Since a square requires right angles AND equal sides, a rhombus without right angles is not a square.

question: How many of these statements are true? (1) All squares are rhombuses. (2) All rhombuses are squares. (3) All rectangles are parallelograms. (4) All parallelograms are rectangles.
hints: Check each statement. Statements 1 and 3 are about going from specific to general.
solution: Two statements are true: (1) All squares are rhombuses — true. (2) All rhombuses are squares — false (rhombuses don't need right angles). (3) All rectangles are parallelograms — true. (4) All parallelograms are rectangles — false (parallelograms don't need right angles).

question: A parallelogram has all sides equal to 7 cm and angles of 60° and 120°. What is the most specific name?
hints: 4 equal sides makes it a rhombus. No right angles, so it's not a square.
solution: It is a rhombus. It has 4 equal sides (rhombus), but its angles are not 90° so it is not a rectangle or square.

question: True or false: A shape can be both a rectangle and a rhombus at the same time.
hints: What shape has both 4 right angles and 4 equal sides?
solution: True. A square is both a rectangle (4 right angles) and a rhombus (4 equal sides) at the same time.
